footer.tagline: Multi-venue prediction market copy trading · Venue-first · Dual-channel execution

home.heading: Multi-venue prediction market
home.subtitle: Product guides, architecture notes, and ops manuals in one place. Built like the Nexus knowledge base: filesystem MDX, three-column docs shell, auto sidebar and TOC.

reach.title: Reach wide
reach.desc: Venue as a first-class citizen. Polymarket first, native multi-venue expansion.

discover.title: Find edge
discover.desc: Leaderboards, performance tags, and cross-venue identity to discover traders worth following.

follow.title: Keep pace
follow.desc: Dual-channel execution: TG delegated signing + self-hosted zero-key daemon.

operate.title: Run ops
operate.desc: Shadow mode, three-tier risk controls, and an admin review desk for a closed ops loop.

docs.title: Docs
docs.subtitle: Unified entry for product and technical docs. Sidebar is generated from the MDX filesystem; articles include reading time, tags, and on-page TOC.
docs.guideDesc: Learn Sharpside’s positioning, dual-channel execution model, and how to discover traders and create follows.
docs.technicalDesc: Setup, architecture, Venue design, integration, and ops manuals for contributors and operators.
docs.localeFallbackNotice: Docs for this language are not available yet; showing Chinese content.

copy.title: Discover traders. Follow in one click.
copy.description: Find addresses worth following via leaderboards and performance tags, then pick a channel and sizing mode to join the signal execution path.
bullets: Leaderboard / trader detail: ROI, win rate, drawdown, and tags at a glance
Create a follow: choose Venue, channel (TG / daemon), and sizing
Trade history & dashboard: copy results stay auditable

channels.title: Dual-channel execution with clear custody boundaries
channels.description: Channel A uses TG delegated signing. Channel B uses a self-hosted zero-key daemon. Both are first-class—pick what fits.
bullets: Channel A: Telegram Bot binds accounts and creates follows; platform signing boundaries are explicit
Channel B: local daemon + API key; private keys never leave the user’s device
One account can mix channels across different follows

venues.title: Venue as a first-class citizen
venues.description: Polymarket ships first. Market mapping, cross-venue identity, and Registry leave a unified interface for more venues later.
bullets: venue-hub: ingest, mapping, identity, performance materialization
Admin reviews mappings and identity to reduce bad follows

risk.title: Ops-ready: risk controls and shadow mode
risk.description: Loss streaks circuit-break, tier/user/Venue overrides, slippage protection, plus shadow mode vs third-party metrics.
bullets: copier runs risk checks before execution: caps, slippage, circuit breakers
Shadow mode: internal performance vs third-party diff → metric_audit
Admin: tag rules, visibility, hotkeys, and a review desk

tech.title: Technical entry for contributors and operators
tech.description: Rust workspace, Gateway BFF, venue-hub, and dual-channel execution. Local setup and integration runbooks live in the docs.
bullets: Architecture: service topology and module roles
Runbook: Polymarket integration, TG Bot, shadow mode
